name: qwen3-pm-orchestration-golden
description: Short Qwen3-TTS source texts for offline ASR A/B scoring in a project-management / multi-agent-orchestration voice context. @ops48-orchestrator 2026-06-02 PDT -- Ground truth is the text field; PR/issue numbers and metrics are written as spoken words for stable WER. Phrasing is grounded in real operator<->agent chat interactions (driving PRs to merge, review verdicts, agent directives, metric readouts, domain jargon). Complements qwen3-call-center-golden; reuses the same asr-golden-ab harness.

samples.text: What's the result from the testing?
Who's running what, and where?
What's the status of the matrix re-run?
Do we have the word error rate numbers yet?
What is blocking the moonshine cell?
Where are the reviewers right now?
How many review rounds did this take?
What is left before we can close this out?
Merge the pull request into the feature branch.
Drive this pull request to merge.
Push the fix and open a pull request.
Commit the change on your working branch.
Do not merge anything to main.
Post the closeout to the issue and stop the loop.
Re-run the full matrix with the trailing silence pad.
Hold the merge for human review.
Drive pull request three seven seven to merge.
Close issue three seven one as resolved.
Pull request three six three was merged already.
Re-open issue three seven zero after the fix.
Review pull request three seven six before merging.
Link pull request three seven seven to issue three seven one.
Issue three six four is closed and done.
Check the build on pull request three seven five.
Both reviewers approved, it is ok to merge.
Round one came back as needs work.
The blocker is resolved and it is ready to merge.
Continuous integration is green on the latest commit.
The reviewer found one issue with the encoder.
Round two is a clean approval from both reviewers.
The verdict is needs work, please address the comments.
All checks passed, go ahead and merge.
Tell codex to commit the fix and open a pull request.
Have opus forty eight review the design and relay the verdict.
Ask the implementer to re-run the matrix.
Recruit a reviewer on a graphics card host.
Stand by until the next pull request is ready.
Restart the codex session, it is stuck in a loop.
Send a bare enter to submit the message.
Acknowledge that you are starting the task.
The word error rate is twenty four percent.
Latency is five hundred thirty five milliseconds at the median.
Kroko is thirty eight percent and sherpa is twenty four percent.
Whisper drops to seventy percent on phone digits.
The matrix is seventy two samples across four backends.
That is five hundred seventy six total runs.
Trailing silence averaged ninety two milliseconds.
Accuracy on emitted words was ninety two percent.
The zipformer needs trailing silence to flush the final chunk.
Check the codec round trip for the mu-law encoder.
The streaming transcriber contract makes the backend swappable.
Endpointing and contextual bias come after the model lever.
We statically link the onnx runtime for the gateway.
Feed the audio buffer through ingest before calling finish.
The pulse code modulation lane was not bit exact.
Resample the narrowband audio back to sixteen kilohertz.
